---
title: "Notes: Session 6: Machine learning I"
format: html
---

| Time (min) | Duration | Topic                           | Additional materials |
|------------|----------|---------------------------------|----------------------|
| 0–23       | 23       | Foundations                     |                      |
| 23–46      | 23       | The Generalization Problem      |                      |
| 46–68      | 22       | The Machine Learning Workflow   |                      |
| 68–90      | 22       | Performance                     |                      |

::: callout-note

Note: moved unsupervised ML (clustering) to EDA (mention here only as an example of unsupervised ML)

TODO: suggest code reading/understanding exercises (e.g., "what are the next steps of the procedure?", "annotate what is done in the code blocks", "give pseudo code", "are there any errors (e.g., switched code blocks)?")

Nice introduction:
https://kuleshov-group.github.io/aml-website (careful: copyright!)

Feature engineering:
https://www.kaggle.com/learn/feature-engineering

Lecture:
https://harvard-iacs.github.io/2019-CS109A/lectures/lecture7/presentation/Lecture7_ModeLSelectionRegularization.pdf


Nice "whats wrong with the procedure": https://courses.cs.washington.edu/courses/cse446/26sp/schedule/lectures/05-train%20test%20split%20cross%20validation.pdf

Exercises in jupyter:
https://github.com/intro-stat-learning/ISLP_labs/
https://harvard-iacs.github.io/2019-CS109A/labs/lab9/notebook/

:::

## TODO: Group work topics

Check which topics were selected by groups, identify potential overlap and guide topic selection.
